name: Louis Prima
abstract: Louis Leo Prima (; December 7, 1910 – August 24, 1978) was an American trumpeter, singer, entertainer, and bandleader. While rooted in New Orleans jazz, swing music, and jump blues, Prima touched on various genres throughout his career: he formed a seven-piece New Orleans–style jazz band in the late 1920s, fronted a swing combo in the 19…

about_quotes.speaker: Georg Brunis
about_quotes.evidence: So I said, well, I got just a guy for you, Louis Prima. So we had Prima and a piano player... So we'd go and then we'd pack the joint.

about_quotes.speaker: Godfrey Hirsch
about_quotes.evidence: I first went to California. July the 17th, 1937. Uh huh. With Louis Prima. Louis Prima's band.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Bourgeois
about_quotes.evidence: Federico and Castang, myself. Played with Louis quite a while here.

about_quotes.speaker: Godfrey Hirsch
about_quotes.evidence: when I first, when I went to California with Louis Prima in 1937

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Nat was one of his early heroes... He said he would stand outside and listen to Prima on 52nd Street... It was through Nat that I wound up meeting Louis [Armstrong]. First I met Hot Lips Page... Nat introduced me to a very nice girl named Jeann Failows... She was, needless to say, close to Louis...
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Louie Bellson mentions Louis Prima in the context of Stan Irwin getting him and Keely Smith to the Sahara, and later lists him as being present at an event ('Joya Sherill was there, and Louis Prima'), but does not describe a direct personal or professional relationship between himself and Prima.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
